sections.summary: Pseudoephedrine combined with the MAOI phenelzine can trigger a hypertensive crisis and is considered contraindicated.
sections.mechanism: Phenelzine inhibits monoamine oxidase, preventing breakdown of norepinephrine, while pseudoephedrine promotes norepinephrine release from nerve terminals. The result is an exaggerated, unchecked surge in circulating norepinephrine.
sections.clinical_effects: Severe, abrupt hypertension can occur, accompanied by headache, palpitations, and risk of intracranial hemorrhage or stroke in serious cases.
sections.management: Avoid pseudoephedrine and other sympathomimetic decongestants entirely in patients taking phenelzine or other MAOIs; recommend non-sympathomimetic alternatives for nasal congestion, such as saline irrigation or intranasal corticosteroids.
sections.monitoring: Not applicable given the combination should be avoided; if accidental exposure occurs, monitor blood pressure urgently and treat hypertensive crisis per standard protocols.

evidence.citation: Nardil (phenelzine) FDA prescribing information, Contraindications and Warnings section
